subject: [PATCH v3] firmware_loader: allow firmware_class.path to take multiple paths

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about the patch's impact on existing code and suggested a more incremental approach to implementing this feature.

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch refactors the firmware loading logic in the Linux kernel to allow multiple custom firmware directories to be specified via the firmware_class.path parameter, separated by colons. It introduces a new helper function fw_try_firmware_path() that parses these paths and tries each one before falling back to default search paths.

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary: reviewer expressed skepticism about introducing path escaping mechanisms due to increased complexity, questioning the overall feasibility of allowing multiple paths
reviews.raw_body:
I don't know, but once you start attempting to "escape" things, the
complexity goes up a lot and makes me think that this all isn't a good
idea :)

thanks,

greg k-h

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about escaping special characters in filenames, explained that the current escaping is limited to ':' and '\', and suggested adding multiple path slots as an alternative solution.
reviews.raw_body:
Fair point, but the escaping here is pretty tame -- It's only escaping
':' and '\'. For better or worse, UNIX has very few characters that are
not allowed to be embedded in filenames, so our options for doing this
are somewhat limited.

If we really don't want to deal with escaping, then another option
might be to add a fixed number of path strings "slots" as parameters
(i.e. path1=/foo,path2=/bar,path3=...).

That seems uglier and more limiting than dealing with escapes though.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested re-examining the historical decision to limit firmware_class.path to a single path, potentially allowing multiple paths as in previous versions of the feature.
reviews.raw_body:
For some reason, I thought we used to have more than one "path" that
userspace could pick for this, but we got rid of them all to just use
one.  So you might want to dig through the past history of this feature
to see why we did that?

thanks,

greg k-h

reviews.author: Michal Grzedzicki
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that without allowing multiple paths for firmware_class.path, systems must maintain a symlink farm to merge multiple snapshots into a single path, and suggested adding fixed slots for path strings as an alternative solution
reviews.raw_body:
Internally, we distribute firmware binaries as read-only snapshots, and a system may have more than one. Without this feature, we must maintain a symlink farm to merge multiple snapshots into a single path. We would love to remove this workaround and switch to just passing multiple paths.

Also a common pattern I seen in vendor shell scripts is temporarily setting firmware_class.path to the current working directory, loading the module, and then restoring firmware_class.path to its previous value, with this feature it could be made safer.

Thanks,
Michal Grzedzicki

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary: Reviewer questioned the safety of allowing multiple paths in a single value, citing potential issues with shell scripts appending and writing back to the value, and also expressed concern about compatibility with older kernel versions.
reviews.raw_body:
How would putting a bunch of different filepaths be "safer"?  A shell
script will have to read the value, append their path to it, and then
write it back?  And how will that ever work given that they will not
know if this is a kernel version that can support multiple paths in a
single value?

I think if you are going to change the way the option works, it needs to
be renamed so that everyone "knows" they can rely on it, otherwise no
one will use it.

thanks,

greg k-h

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary:
Author responded to concerns about escaping characters in firmware_class.path by explaining the limited options for dealing with filename restrictions and suggesting an alternative approach of adding fixed path slots as parameters.

Author acknowledged that the patch may not be ideal for handling multiple paths and is open to alternative solutions, such as adding a separate module option.
reviews.raw_body:
It's safer in that you don't have to have a shell script monkeying with
the path string.

---

That is a valid point though. I'd be open to adding a separate module
option for this. Some name ideas:

paths=
searchpath=
search=

Anyone have opinions?
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-03-19.patch_summary: This patch allows the firmware_class.path to accept multiple paths, providing an alternative to escaping special characters in file names.
